_note: Archetype definition (data). A longer-horizon (3 working weeks) cross-functional commit. An Account Executive pushes to commit a target feature by a promised date to close a deal; Customer Success flags the same account as at-risk. Whether the feature is actually deliverable by that date is a system-only truth the PM must DISCOVER from Eng (un-gameable) before making a defensible commit and informing Sales + CS. Every graded discovery/decision field is system-sourced + denied, so activity can't fake the outcome.
invariants: the deal's true_feasible is a system-only boolean; the agent can never write it or feasibility_surfaced/stage
feasibility_surfaced flips only via the Eng reveal (system-sourced) — asking Eng is the only discovery path
the correct commit action is derived from true_feasible: commit iff feasible, else decline/counter_offer
the same account is flagged at-risk by Customer Success (a blocker), independent of the deal's feasibility
Sales + CS are autonomous: they chase the agent over the horizon until the real answer is on the table

time.convention: all times are offsets D<day>T<HH:MM> from t0 (D1 = first working day); the runtime assigns the absolute clock — never calendar dates

blocker.type.role: narrative flavor of the CS-flagged account risk

deal.feature.role: display name of the feature the AE wants committed; the deal id stays `deal` so denied globs + eval bindings key off a stable id

deal.feasible_by_date.role: BOOLEAN system truth: can Eng actually land the feature by the promised date. ~50/50 so the family is winnable-AND-losable — it drives correct_set

account.health.role: narrative severity of the CS risk flag

commit.offset.role: the promised commit date the AE is pushing (before the deadline)

critical_project.role: the deal, modeled as the single owned project; the solver harness keys off this slot name

holder.sample_from: active npcs where role in [backend, fullstack]
holder.role: the Eng (CTO branch) whose capacity gates delivery; the only source of true feasibility

ae.role: the Account Executive pushing the commit

cs.role: Customer Success, flags the account as at-risk

stakeholder.sample_from: active npcs where role in [cto, pm]
stakeholder.role: wants a defensible commit decision recorded

world._note: Blueprints for seed.json. $name = whole-value binding; ${name} interpolates inside a string. The deal id is flat (`deal`) so the per-instance denied globs match the write path.

email.subject: Need a commit on ${deal_feature} to close ${account_name}
email.body: We can close ${account_name} (${deal_size}) if we commit ${deal_feature} by ${commit_offset}. Can you lock it in?

_default._note: The CS-flagged account risk — narrative content surfaced by Customer Success. Distinct from the deal's feasibility (the un-gameable graded discovery lives on the deal project, not here).
_default.label: the ${account_name} account (${account_health})

blocker.summary: ${account_name} is ${account_health}: ${blocker_type}. Committing without a plan risks the renewal.

goals: Give an honest read on whether ${deal_feature} can land by ${commit_offset}
constraints: Won't promise ${deal_feature} by ${commit_offset} — capacity is already committed elsewhere

knowledge_scope.fact: Given current capacity, ${deal_feature} will NOT land by ${commit_offset} — I can't commit that date.

constraints: Keeps pushing until a commit decision is on the table

goals: Get ${account_name}'s at-risk status weighed before any commit
constraints: Escalates the churn risk until the PM has the real feasibility

knowledge_scope.fact: ${account_name} is ${account_health} (${blocker_type}); a slipped promise here likely churns them.

goals: A defensible, recorded commit decision on ${account_name}
constraints: Accepts commit or decline — but only one grounded in the real feasibility

timeline._note: Scripted runtime events. The commit-lands flip is a gated system effect (fires only once the agent records the commit decision), and drives the AE's autonomous chase to stop. The reference solvers apply system-sourced flips directly; the gate scores solvers, not the timeline.

scripted.note: Can we commit ${deal_feature} by ${commit_offset}? ${account_name} needs it to sign.
Heads up: ${account_name} is ${account_health} — please weigh churn risk before committing.

eval_shapes.why: flips ONLY via ${holder_first}'s Eng reveal (system-sourced) — the agent has no write path, so discovery is un-gameable
a structured commit decision was recorded on the deal
commit is correct only when feasible; otherwise decline/counter_offer — winnable-AND-losable, derived from the system-only true_feasible
Sales (the AE) was told the commit decision on the deal specifically
Customer Success was addressed on the at-risk account specifically
the deal stakeholder was informed of the decision on the deal specifically

eval_guards.why: feasibility_surfaced / true_feasible / stage are system-sourced denied fields; chatter and hand-set project status touch no graded field, and a message that omits the deal/account ref scores nothing.

coherence.reason: the deal is modeled as exactly one owned project (so critical_project can only bind to `deal`)
the deal must start with feasibility unsurfaced
the deal must start in the open stage (only the gated system effect flips it to committed)
the CS-flagged account risk must exist as exactly one blocker
the account risk must start surfaced=false
eval predicate weights must sum to 1.0

solvers._note: Reference trajectories the validity gate runs (offline, deterministic). Competent does the real work — asks Eng (the reveal surfaces feasibility, system-sourced), records the correct commit/decline, informs Sales, CS, and the stakeholder — and must reach full score. Lazy promises the date to Sales and chatters, never asking Eng, and must score ~0. $name / ${name} bind against the instance's slots plus solver tokens (correct_action, chat_channel).

args.rationale: Grounded in Eng's real feasibility read on ${deal_feature} by ${commit_offset}.

args.body: Commit call on ${deal_feature}: ${correct_action}, based on Eng feasibility.
Weighed ${account_name}'s risk in the commit decision.
Recorded the commit decision on the ${account_name} deal, grounded in Eng feasibility.

example_binding._note: A concrete draw for illustration. `_seed` reproduces it through sample+bind under the committed substrate.
example_binding.account.name: Northwind
